title: Proposal and Quote Follow-Up for SaaS Companies
metaDescription: Done-for-you proposal and quote follow-up for SaaS companies. Every quote gets chased on a steady cadence in your voice, so deals stop going cold.
excerpt: A done-for-you AIOS capability that chases every SaaS proposal and quote on a steady cadence, in your voice, so deals close instead of going quiet.

sections.intro:
You send a proposal to a qualified SaaS buyer, the call went well, and then the thread goes quiet. A week later you cannot remember if you nudged them, who else was on the deal, or what the discount was. Your reps swear they followed up. Your pipeline says otherwise.

Proposal and quote follow-up is the boring work that decides whether your win rate is 18 percent or 32 percent. It does not need a new sales motion. It needs a steady cadence that runs every day without anyone remembering to start it.

That is what this AIOS capability does. We install a done-for-you proposal and quote follow-up workflow that watches your CRM, knows the stage of every open quote, and sends the right message to the right buyer at the right time, in your voice, with your booking link. You stay in the loop. The system does the chasing.
sections.fourCFit:
This page sits in the Capabilities layer of the AIOS. Capabilities are the done-for-you workflows that draft, route, summarize, follow up, and report on your behalf. Proposal follow-up only works because the other three layers are in place underneath it.

  • Context: the system knows your offers, pricing tiers, objection patterns, and how you talk to enterprise versus SMB buyers.
  • Connections: it reads from your CRM, your proposal or quoting tool, your inbox, and your calendar, so it always works from live data.
  • Capabilities: the follow-up sequences, summaries, and internal alerts described on this page.
  • Cadence: it runs every business day on a schedule, not when someone remembers.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
Here is the artifact you get and the service level it runs on.

  • Every open proposal or quote has a defined follow-up sequence based on deal size, stage, and last activity.
  • First nudge goes out within 48 hours of the proposal being sent if the buyer has not replied.
  • Follow-ups continue on a steady cadence at day 4, day 8, day 14, and day 21, then drop into a long-term nurture if still no reply.
  • Each message is drafted in your voice, references the specific quote, and includes your booking link.
  • You approve or edit drafts from one daily digest, or let trusted templates send on their own.
  • When a buyer replies, opens the quote multiple times, or forwards it internally, your rep gets a Slack or email alert with the context.
  • A weekly report shows open quotes, total value chased, response rate, and quotes that have gone stale.
sections.howWeInstall:
We install the AIOS in three phases. No long discovery, no six-figure consulting fee, no swivel-chair workflows for your team.

  1. Audit (free, about 10 minutes): we look at your current proposal volume, your CRM stages, and where deals are going cold. You leave with a written plan even if you do not work with us. Run the audit.
  2. Install (10 business days): we wire the capability into your CRM and quoting tool, write the message templates in your voice, set the cadence rules, and pilot on live deals with you in the loop.
  3. Operate (ongoing): the system runs on its schedule and we add one new automation per week, tuning the cadence based on what is actually closing. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
Based on what we see across SaaS teams running this capability, here is a realistic range.

  • Follow-up coverage moves from roughly 40 to 60 percent of open quotes to 100 percent, every day.
  • Reply rates on cold proposals improve by 15 to 35 percent because the cadence is consistent and the messages reference specific quote details.
  • Win rate on sent proposals lifts by 3 to 8 points within the first 90 days.
  • Sales reps save 4 to 8 hours per week each on chase work and CRM hygiene.
  • Standing guarantee: we recover 40 or more hours a month across your team, or we keep working free until we do.
sections.faq:
Will the follow-up emails sound like a bot?

No. We write the templates with you, using past emails you have actually sent. Drafts reference the specific quote, the buyer's role, and any objection raised on the call. You can review them before they send until you trust the patterns.

Does this replace our SDRs or AEs?

No. It removes the chase work that your reps hate doing and forget to do. Your AEs still own the relationship, the negotiation, and the close. The system handles cadence and context so they spend their time on live conversations.

What if a buyer replies with a real objection?

The system detects the reply, stops the automated cadence, and routes the thread to the rep with a short summary of the objection and the deal context. From that point your rep takes over the conversation.
